preview_text:
The model rates France as the favorite with a 41% win probability, while England's chances stand at 28%. The gap reflects a modest edge rather than a dominant superiority; the underdog remains competitive within the forecast range.

The draw is substantive at 31%, making this a three-way contest. France's advantage compresses when draws are included—the model suggests roughly equal cumulative chances for either team to avoid defeat, indicating a closely matched encounter rather than a lopsided affair.

The sportsbook consensus leans more heavily toward a France victory at 47%, a 5-point gap above the model. Both sources agree England is roughly a 27–28% proposition and treat draws as secondary outcomes. The divergence on France's win probability is modest but meaningful; the dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is more accurate.
